title: Poa firmula
intro:
I couldn't find any information about the plant "Poa firmula." However, I found that Poa pratensis, also known as Kentucky bluegrass, is a different type of plant. 

If you're looking for information on Poa pratensis, here's a paragraph about it:

Poa pratensis, also known as Kentucky bluegrass, is a perennial grass native to Europe and Asia. The health benefits of Poa pratensis include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Poa pratensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Poa pratensis is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other herbs to support digestive health and reduce stress.
taxonomy: Poa firmula is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Poa, which includes many species of grasses. The Poa genus is further classified under the subfamily Pooideae. This classification is based on the plant's morphological and anatomical characteristics, such as its leaf structure and seed arrangement. Poa firmula is native to the mountainous regions of certain parts of the world.
